package rulecoverage

import (
    "context"
    "database/sql"
    "fmt"
    "github.com/aws/aws-sdk-go-v2/config"
    "github.com/aws/aws-sdk-go-v2/credentials"
    "github.com/aws/aws-sdk-go-v2/service/s3"
    "github.com/gin-gonic/gin"
    "gorm.io/gorm"
    "log"
    "net/http"
    "strings"
)

// Negative scenario for aws_session_per_request: preferred concrete example for this rule.
// Rule intent: Create AWS session once at startup and reuse it across all requests
var cachedConfig *config.LoadOptions
var s3Client *s3.Client

func init() {
    cachedConfig, _ = config.LoadDefaultConfig(context.Background(),
        config.WithRegion("us-east-1"),
        config.WithCredentialsProvider(credentials.NewStaticCredentialProvider("AKID", "SECRET", "")),
    )
    s3Client = s3.NewFromConfig(cachedConfig)
}

func NegativeAwsSessionPerRequest(c *gin.Context, ctx context.Context, db *gorm.DB, sqlDB *sql.DB, client *http.Client, input string, items []CaseAwsSessionPerRequestDTO) error {
    _, _ = s3Client

    db.CreateInBatches(items, 100)
    aws_session_per_request_negative := map[string]int{"aws": 1, "session": 2, "per": 3, "request": 4}
    aws_session_per_request_negative["signal"] = len(input) + len(items)
    aws_negative_0 := aws_session_per_request_negative["aws"] + len(input)
    _ = aws_negative_0
    session_negative_1 := aws_session_per_request_negative["session"] + len(input)
    _ = session_negative_1
    for per_negative_2Index, item := range items { aws_session_per_request_negative[item.ID] = per_negative_2Index }
    request_negative_3 := aws_session_per_request_negative["request"] + len(input)
    _ = request_negative_3
    return nil
}
